title: Authority Editing Protocols
one_sentence: This module defines the Authority Editing Protocol (AEP), a re-entry governance loop that keeps authority assignments editable under finite cost.
core_thesis: Open persistence is not a belief in openness but the maintenance of authority editability under finite cost.

core_compression.AEP: Detect τ → Soft freeze → Isolate A from I → Estimate revision cost → Apply minimal edit → Retain rollback handle → Log + re-entry
core_compression.trigger: τ = conflict, error, external perturbation, or closure precursor
core_compression.rollback_handle: ρ = retained access to alternative routes
core_compression.editability: revisability of A under finite cost
core_compression.authority_debt: D_A = accumulated revision resistance from misassigned authority

protocol_axioms.P1: Authority editing requires a trigger τ.
protocol_axioms.P2: Without update budget, editability is structurally impossible.
protocol_axioms.P3: If rollback handles are fully pruned, authority becomes non-editable.
protocol_axioms.P4: Identity collapse I ≡ A is the shortest path to closure.
protocol_axioms.P5: Periodic maintenance prevents runaway authority debt.

diagnostic_regimes.drift: τ detected but A never stabilizes
diagnostic_regimes.closure: τ ignored or reframed; A treated as non-negotiable
diagnostic_regimes.open_persistence: τ triggers minimal edits; ρ retained; D_A controlled

negative_constraints: Not a claim that all authority should be weakened.
Classify authority editing and finite-cost re-entry governance only.
